name: Enterprise Deployment
headline: Create a high-trust path for teams that need self-hosting, governance, and rollout support
idealFor: Best for internal platform teams, agencies with strict clients, and compliance-minded buyers
description: Enterprise Deployment gives VoiceToWebsite a credible higher-ticket lane. It focuses on self-hosting, controlled rollout, support, and deeper integration for organizations that need more than a standard SaaS experience.
bullets: Offer self-hosted deployment and infrastructure control
Support security, governance, and operational review
Create larger contract sizes and longer retention

qualificationChecks.title: Governance and approvals
qualificationChecks.description: Confirm where the system runs, how actions are approved, and how operational risk is managed.

qualificationChecks.title: Deployment scope
qualificationChecks.description: Clarify whether the buyer needs self-hosting, custom rollout support, or deeper integration into internal processes.

qualificationChecks.title: Commercial fit
qualificationChecks.description: Only move into a formal commercial path once the hosting, security, and implementation shape is understood.

timeline.title: Qualify the environment
timeline.description: Establish hosting, security, and governance requirements before proposing a final delivery path.

timeline.title: Define the rollout
timeline.description: Map the deployment, support, and customization work required to make the system fit the organization.

timeline.title: Move into scoped deployment
timeline.description: Turn the qualification conversation into a serious rollout plan once the fit is clear.

proofPoints.title: Qualification-first instead of forced checkout
proofPoints.description: Enterprise buyers need a review path that respects security, governance, and rollout complexity.

proofPoints.title: Raises the value of every smaller offer
proofPoints.description: A credible enterprise lane makes the whole business feel more serious, even for buyers who ultimately choose smaller plans.

sections.title: Control and governance
sections.description: Enterprise buyers care about where the system runs, who can approve actions, and how changes are tracked.

sections.title: Customization and onboarding
sections.description: More complex teams need help connecting the platform to their processes, repos, and ways of working.

sections.title: Strategic value
sections.description: A credible enterprise path raises the value of the whole business, even for buyers who start on smaller plans.

leadForm.heading: Submit the enterprise qualification brief
leadForm.intro: Use this form to outline hosting, governance, rollout, and customization requirements before anyone tries to force this path through a generic checkout.

cta.heading: Use enterprise deployment to qualify larger and more durable deals.
cta.body: This offer should move through a serious qualification path first so the eventual commercial scope is grounded in reality.
